howItWorks.intro: This espresso extraction calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: What is the ideal espresso ratio?
faq.answer: Most specialty coffee uses a 1:2 ratio (e.g., 18g in, 36g out) in 25-30 seconds. Ristretto is 1:1.5, lungo is 1:3+.

faq.question: Is the Espresso Extraction Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard practical calculation formulas trusted by users. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

faq.question: What dose should I enter?
faq.answer: Enter the most accurate dose value you have available. If you're estimating, use a conservative figure. You can always run the calculator again with different values to see how changes affect the results.

faq.question: Can I use this calculator on my phone?
faq.answer: Yes, the Espresso Extraction Calculator is fully responsive and works on any device — phone, tablet, or desktop. No app download needed.

sections.title: What Is the Espresso Extraction Calculator?
sections.body: The Espresso Extraction Calculator is a free online tool designed for users who need quick, accurate calculations in the practical calculation space. By entering your dose, yield, shot time, you get instant results including brew ratio, flow rate, assessment. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting brew ratio right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In practical calculation, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by users worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to get accurate results with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your dose and need to find the right brew ratio. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Dose (grams)
howToSteps.description: Type or select your dose in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Yield (grams)
howToSteps.description: Type or select your yield in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Shot Time (seconds)
howToSteps.description: Type or select your shot time in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your brew ratio, flow rate, assessment. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your dose before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both brew ratio and flow rate — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to get accurate results.
If you're unsure about your shot time, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
